build_reproducibility.details: CRITICAL: Published site (git HEAD docs/index.html) differs from freshly-built HTML in 14 locations. Changes: (1) sections.json part2: DOI corrections for Livermore 2020 (s41586-020-2018-1 → s41561-020-0570-9) and Finlay 2016 (nature21807 → s40623-016-0486-1); (2) sections.json part4: Section 4.1 TLDR text update — '9 of 83 registry items (69 confirmed, 4 refined, 10 prospective)' → '9 of the site's 69+ claims'; (3) sections.json part6: substantial new analytical content added for WIN-058b storm corollary (κ-inverse implication analysis, quantified ΔB premise); (4) generate-html.js: dark-mode CSS selector removed, aria-level attribute removed, caption conditional supplement-count span added, status labels updated ('0/69 met', '1 partial' eclipse status), link handler simplified.

summary: Overall status FAIL due to critical build drift. The published GitHub Pages site is running an outdated version — workspace build-scripts/generate-html.js and data/sections.json have been modified since the last git commit but not pushed. Key missing content: DOI corrections for two peer-reviewed papers (Livermore 2020, Finlay 2016) in Part 2; updated Section 4.1 TLDR removing stale registry-item counts; substantial new WIN-058b storm corollary analysis in Part 6 (κ-inverse implication with quantified ΔB premise). Fix requires committing both files and running node build.js publish from the clean clone. The expansion-tracker next_id collision issue (384<=388) is worsening — yesterday's alert (365<=369) was not actioned by the decider, and the batch writer created 5 more EXPs without advancing next_id. This will cause an overwrite collision on next analyst allocation; patch next_id → 389 immediately. Four orphaned EXP output files (308, 311, 322, 323) need tracker entries. Good news: the 42 terminal-items-in-live issue from yesterday is fully resolved. test.js at 3611 passed / 0 failed (+5 from yesterday). Curmudgeon digest clean, drift audit fresh, discoverability infrastructure intact.
